arts.ttl: Wilson Odobert double takes Tottenham past AZ to keep season alive
arts.athr: Ed Aarons at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou can breathe again. The Spurs manager had been hoping that someone would step up to save their season and his salvation arrived in the shape of Wilson Odobert, with a little bit of help from James Maddison. When the England midfielder made it 2-0 early in the second half to add to Odobert’s well-taken first goal for the club, it looked like Postecoglou’s side were well on their way to a last eight showdown with Eintracht Frankfurt. This being Tottenham, however, things are never that simple and a mistake from Odobert allowed Peer Koopmeiners to level on aggregate with 20 minute to play. But Odobert kept Postecoglou’s hopes of ending the season with some silverware alive when he swept home at the end of a flowing move instigated by Maddison to send Spurs into the quarter-finals of the Europa League for the first time since 2013. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Is Ange-ball back? Tottenham optimism return as battling Europa League win keeps season alive
arts.sum: Pivotal win can instil fresh belief in the players and supporters ahead of quarter-final

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou’s bold call saves Tottenham season to extend Europa League run
arts.sum: A 3-1 win for Spurs in north London sets up a last-eight tie with Eintracht Frankfurt

arts.ttl: Tottenham 3-1 AZ Alkmaar (3-2 agg): Highlights, man of the match, stats as Wilson Odobert writes Europa League hero arc
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur's hopes of ending a 17-year trophy drought remain alive thanks to a 3-2 aggregate win over AZ Alkmaar in their Europa League last-16 tie.

arts.ttl: Tottenham fans are all making the same point about Lucas Bergvall after beating AZ to reach Europa League quarter-finals
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur are through to the Europa League quarter-finals after beating AZ Alkmaar 3-2 on aggregate. Spurs lost 1-0 in the last-16 first leg last...

arts.ttl: Spurs turn around AZ tie to keep trophy hopes alive
arts.sum: Tottenham overcame a one-goal deficit to keep their trophy hopes alive with a 3-1 (3-2 aggregate) win over AZ Alkmaar in north London thanks to Wilson Odobert and James Maddison.

arts.ttl: Tottenham 3-1 AZ Alkmaar (3-2): Wilson Odobert bags a brace as Ange Postecoglou's side overturn first leg deficit to reach Europa League quarter-finals
arts.sum: MATT BARLOW AT THE TOTTENHAM HOTSPUR STADIUM: Anxiety, brilliance, confusion. Perhaps not quite the classic A to Z of Tottenham under Ange Postecoglou.

arts.ttl: Tottenham v AZ: Europa League last-16, second leg – live (plus Rangers and Chelsea updates!)

arts.ttl: Tottenham team vs AZ confirmed - Romero and Van de Ven start with four changes for Spurs - football.london
arts.sum: Vicario; Porro, Romero, Van de Ven, Spence; Sarr, Bergvall, Maddison; Odobert, Solanke, Son. Subs: Austin, Whiteman, Udogie, Davies, Gray, Bissouma, Johnson, Tel, Moore, Scarlett. For this game, Spurs are without Kevin Danso, who joins the injury list, but Ben Davies is back in training and Cristian Romero and Micky van de Ven both start after coming through their returns on Sunday.

arts.ttl: 'To be honest': Mauricio Pochettino gives his verdict on one day returning to Tottenham
